title: OpenAI and ChatGPT: advances in health, scheduled tasks, market share drop, and IPO preparations
summary: OpenAI strengthens ChatGPT with health features and scheduled tasks, while its market share drops below 50% and the company prepares for its IPO.

title: Adobe integrates artificial intelligence agents into its Creative Cloud applications
summary: The company incorporates AI assistants and agents into Photoshop, Premiere, Illustrator, and InDesign to automate creative tasks.

title: Apple faces price increases due to memory shortage and opens the App Store to competition in Brazil
summary: Apple's CEO confirms that iPhone and MacBook prices will rise due to the memory crisis, while the company allows alternative app stores in Brazil.

title: Noam Shazeer leaves Google DeepMind to join OpenAI
summary: The co-leader of Gemini at Google DeepMind returns to OpenAI after two years at the company, in a key move in the AI talent war.

title: Midjourney presents a full-body scanner with artificial intelligence
summary: The AI image generation company launches a medical scanner that performs a full body scan in 60 seconds without radiation or magnets.

title: US government accelerates electrical connections for AI data centers
summary: US regulators back Trump's plan to prioritize the grid for artificial intelligence data centers.

title: Bertín Osborne launches his own mobile operator in Spain, Española de Telefonía
summary: The singer and host bursts into the Spanish telecommunications market with a conservative-leaning mobile company.
source: BandaAncha:️ Noticias, guias y foros sobre fibra y móvil

title: Morale at Meta hits rock bottom after layoffs and revolt by AI workers
summary: Meta's CTO admits morale is the worst in history after layoffs, while AI employees rebel against management.

title: SteamOS receives new updates with performance improvements and Steam Machine support
summary: Valve releases versions 3.8 and 3.8.10 of SteamOS, adding a new kernel, updated graphics drivers, and better compatibility with Steam Machine.

title: The SSD retail market collapses with soaring prices and falling sales
summary: SSD prices reach record levels, even exceeding the cost of a PS5, while Silicon Motion confirms the virtual disappearance of the retail market.

title: Chinese memory chips emerge as an alternative to alleviate the global shortage
summary: Facing the supply crisis, China orders its memory manufacturers to prioritize the consumer market, while Chinese chips are shaping up as a salvation for users.

title: Anthropic updates Claude Code and Claude Design with new collaborative features
summary: Claude Code adds Artifacts for sharing live pages, and Claude Design improves design system consistency and integration with Claude Code.

title: Two new daily puzzle games, MapTap and Gerrymandle, gain popularity
summary: MapTap tests geographic knowledge and Gerrymandle challenges to redraw electoral maps, following in the footsteps of Wordle.

title: Trump administration sets rules for artificial intelligence on the fly
summary: The White House establishes AI regulations in real time and President Trump criticizes the country's most promising models.

title: The economic impact of AI generates wealth but also fears of a bubble
summary: While AI promises unprecedented wealth, Yann LeCun warns that AI labs like OpenAI and Anthropic could be in a bubble that will burst.

title: AI in dating and the perception of beauty worry Americans
summary: According to Match, nearly half of singles in the US view AI negatively in dating, while experts warn of the aesthetic pressure generated by these tools.

title: Problems in autonomous driving: Waymo gets into construction sites and Rivian is sued for broken promises
summary: Waymo's autonomous cars drive through active construction zones and Rivian owners report that autonomous features don't deliver on promises.

title: Initiatives to combat mobile addiction: from slow technology to putting the phone in a bag
summary: While slow technology offers solutions to the attention crisis, a town in Huesca experiments with storing phones in bags for nine hours.

title: Big acquisitions in artificial intelligence: Elastic buys DeductiveAI and SpaceX acquires Anysphere
summary: Elastic acquires startup DeductiveAI for up to $85 million, and SpaceX buys the creator of Cursor, Anysphere, for $60 billion.

title: Active malware campaigns: trojans on GitHub and Windows clipper with Tor communication
summary: A researcher discovers 10,000 GitHub repositories distributing trojans, while Microsoft details a Clipper malware campaign that uses USB and Tor for its C2.
